LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S TAX REFUND CLAIM IS SUSTAINED. ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS DEFICIENT BECAUSE THE CITY'S PAYROLL TAX AS APPLIED TO "EQUITY" PARTNERS CAN, BY ITS TERMS, COVERS "COMPENSATION FOR SERVICES" RECEIVED BY SUCH PARTNERS, WHETHER OR NOT SUCH COMPENSATION IS PAID IN THE FORM OF "GUARANTEED PAYMENTS." PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF IT WISHES TO DO SO AND CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO A REFUND BECAUSE THE AMOUNT OF THE PAYMENTS MADE TO ITS EQUITY PARTNERS AS COMPENSATION FOR SERVICES WAS LESS THAN THE AMOUNT OF PAYMENTS MADE TO THOSE PARTNERS THAT FORMED THE BASIS FOR THE TAX ASSESSED AND COLLECTED. THE COMPLAINT AND THE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE MATERIALS DO NOT SHOW THAT PROPOSITION Q VIOLATED PROPOSITION 218 OR SECTION 17041.5. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AMY L. SILVERSTEIN & ATTY. JOHANNA W. ROBERTS OF SILVERSTEIN & POMERANTZ LLP, TEL. NO. 593-3502 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES M. EMERY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4628 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action. Plaintiff's objection to defendants' request for judicial notice of plaintiff's tax refund claim is sustained. All other requests for judicial notice are granted. The first cause of action is deficient because the City's payroll tax as applied to "equity" partners can, by its terms, covers "compensation for services" received by such partners, whether or not such compensation is paid in the form of "guaranteed payments." Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege, if it wishes to do so and can do so in good faith, that it is entitled to a refund because the amount of the payments made to its equity partners as compensation for services was less than the amount of payments made to those partners that formed the basis for the tax assessed and collected. The complaint and the judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violated Proposition 218 or section 17041.5. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2012 Continued To Apr-06-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610
